summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their first regular meeting of 2024, electing new board leadership with Trustee Hodges as chair and Trustee Allen as vice chair. The board approved moving forward with MSP Design as the architect for Fire Station 18, approved a $50,000 ARPA contribution to the Clermont County Land Bank for cleanup of the contaminated Combs dump properties on State Route 28, and conducted several annual housekeeping items. A retirement presentation was made for firefighter Bill Waits for 22 years of service, and nine new part-time firefighter EMTs were hired.
decisions: Adopted the meeting agenda
Approved payment of bills for December 12, 2023 through January 9, 2024
Nominated and approved Trustee Hodges as Board Chair for 2024
Nominated and approved Trustee Allen as Vice Chair for 2024
Adopted the 2024 schedule for regular Board of Trustee meetings (second Tuesdays at 6 PM)
Approved moving forward with negotiating a contract with MSP Design as architect for Station 18
Accepted resignation of firefighter EMT Clayton Newman effective 1-3-24
Hired nine part-time firefighter EMTs at $15.50/hour effective 1-14-2024
Set 2024 standard mileage rate at 67 cents per business mile
Approved sale of surplus items via govdeals.com (Resolution R-001-2024)
Set 2024 zoning commission and BZA member compensation at $50/month, not to exceed $599 annually
Approved 2024 road mileage of 47.062 miles per Clermont County Engineer
Approved refund of $470 in zoning fees to Don Cornwell
Passed Resolution R-002-2024 appointing Trustee Hauserman to Clermont County 9-1-1 program review committee
Increased appropriations in ARPA account 2272-130-322-0001 by $50,000
Approved agreement with Clermont County Land Bank to assume cleanup of Combs dump properties on State Route 28
Approved $50,000 ARPA contribution to Clermont County Land Bank for Combs dump remediation with reimbursement request upon property sale
Adjourned into executive session at 6:35 PM per ORC 121.22(g)(1)
Reappointed Jesse Shaw to the BZA with term expiring December 31, 2028
Moved Steve Pegram from alternate to member of the Zoning Commission with term expiring December 31, 2028
Adjourned at 7:32 PM
action_items: Chief Myers to contact MSP Design to begin contract negotiations for Station 18 architectural services
Prosecutor's office to review MSP Design contract once negotiated
Administrator Rose to have road mileage paperwork signed once received from County Engineer Craig
Legal (Brian) to review the ARPA contribution agreement with the Land Bank
Administrator Rose to sign related documents for Land Bank agreement
Melinda to distribute message to employees about hard copies of ADP tax documents
Chief Rose and team to begin construction manager at risk RFQ process after architect contract is signed
key_amounts: $50,000 ARPA contribution to Clermont County Land Bank for Combs dump cleanup
$3,000,000 estimated cleanup cost for Combs dump properties on State Route 28
$500,000 estimated local match required for brownfield grant above $1,000,000
$1,000,000 tentatively guaranteed to land bank from brownfield funds in 2024
$387,700 brownfield grant received in early 2023 for environmental studies
$100,000 approximate unpaid property taxes on Combs dump properties
$15.50 per hour rate for new part-time firefighter EMTs
$0.67 per mile IRS mileage reimbursement rate for 2024
$470 zoning fee refund to Don Cornwell
$50 per month compensation for zoning commission and BZA members (max $599/year)
notable_topics: Election of 2024 Board Chair (Hodges) and Vice Chair (Allen)
Station 18 fire station architect selection - MSP Design chosen via RFQ process
Combs dump cleanup partnership with Clermont County Land Bank using ARPA funds
Retirement recognition for firefighter Bill Waits (22 years of service)
Hiring of nine part-time firefighter EMTs from Scarlet Oaks adult class
Clermont County 9-1-1 Next Gen (NG911) commission appointment
Brownfield grant rule changes affecting township eligibility
Annual housekeeping items (mileage rate, surplus sales, zoning compensation, road mileage)
Upscale Motors theft case investigation noted by Trustee Hauserman
BZA and Zoning Commission membership changes
attendees: Steve Pegram (Zoning Commission alternate moved to member)
notes: Trustee Hauserman abstained on his own appointment to the 9-1-1 commission despite prosecutor's office saying there was no personal gain issue. The BZA reappointment for Jesse Shaw states term expiring December 31, 2022, which appears to be a misspoken date likely meant to be 2028. Terry Settle declined reappointment to Zoning Commission due to his business success. Executive session held from 6:35 PM to 7:30 PM regarding appointment/discipline/dismissal of a public employee with no public action taken afterward. The Combs dump properties have been a township issue for approximately 17 years. Inside Edition reportedly approached about the Upscale Motors theft case. Panama City tornado was mentioned with concern for staff member Katie's safety.
title: BOT 1-9-24
